seoTitle: Panasonic Capacitors & Relays | Product Catalog | Authorized Distributor
seoDescription: Complete catalog of Panasonic capacitors and relays. Aluminum electrolytic, film capacitors, electromechanical and solid state relays. Technical specifications and selection guide.

faqs.question: What types of capacitors does Panasonic offer?
faqs.answer: Panasonic offers a comprehensive range of capacitor technologies to meet diverse application requirements. Their aluminum electrolytic capacitors include radial lead, surface mount (SMD), and snap-in types for power supply filtering, energy storage, and DC bus applications. Panasonic's film capacitors feature metallized polypropylene and polyester constructions for AC filtering, snubber circuits, and power factor correction. They also offer electric double-layer capacitors (EDLC) for energy storage and backup power applications. All capacitor lines feature Panasonic's proprietary materials and manufacturing processes that ensure high reliability, long operational life, and excellent electrical performance across temperature ranges.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our capacitor categories below to explore Panasonic's complete capacitor portfolio, or contact our FAE team for application-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: What relay types are available from Panasonic?
faqs.answer: Panasonic manufactures a wide variety of relay types for different switching applications. Their electromechanical relays include power relays for high-current switching, signal relays for low-level switching, and automotive relays for vehicle applications. These relays feature various contact configurations (SPST, SPDT, DPDT), coil voltages, and mounting styles. Panasonic's solid state relays (SSRs) provide silent, fast switching with no mechanical wear, ideal for high-speed or high-reliability applications. They also offer latching relays for low-power applications and high-frequency relays for RF applications. All Panasonic relays are designed with high-quality contact materials and robust construction to ensure long electrical life and reliable operation in demanding environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Explore our relay categories to find the right relay type for your switching application, or consult our FAE team for relay selection guidance.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Panasonic capacitor for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Panasonic capacitor requires evaluating several key parameters. First, determine the required capacitance value based on your circuit requirements such as ripple current, hold-up time, or filtering needs. Next, select a voltage rating with appropriate derating - typically 20-30% above your maximum operating voltage. Consider the operating temperature range and select a series rated for your application's temperature extremes. For switching power supplies, calculate the ripple current requirements and select a capacitor with adequate ripple current rating and thermal characteristics. Lifetime requirements are critical - Panasonic offers standard, long-life, and ultra-long-life series. Physical constraints such as size, mounting type (radial, SMD, snap-in), and lead spacing must also be considered. Our FAE team can assist with detailed calculations and selection guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our online selection tools or contact our FAE team with your application specifications for personalized component recommendations.

faqs.question: What factors should I consider when selecting Panasonic relays?
faqs.answer: When selecting Panasonic relays, several key factors must be considered to ensure reliable operation. First, determine the load type and characteristics - resistive, inductive, or capacitive loads have different switching requirements. Calculate the required switching current and voltage, including any inrush currents for inductive loads. Select the appropriate contact configuration (SPST, SPDT, DPDT) based on your circuit requirements. Consider the coil voltage and ensure it matches your control circuit. For high-reliability applications, evaluate the relay's electrical life rating and contact material. Environmental factors such as temperature, vibration, and humidity should also be considered. Panasonic offers relays with various contact materials (AgSnO2, AgCdO, Au-clad) optimized for different load types. Our FAE team can provide detailed relay selection guidance based on your specific application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team with your load characteristics and switching requirements for personalized relay recommendations.

faqs.question: Does Panasonic offer AEC-Q200 qualified capacitors for automotive?
faqs.answer: Yes, Panasonic offers a comprehensive portfolio of AEC-Q200 qualified capacitors specifically designed for automotive applications. Their automotive-grade aluminum electrolytic capacitors feature extended temperature ratings up to 150C, enhanced vibration resistance, and long operational life required by automotive standards. These capacitors undergo rigorous qualification testing including temperature cycling, mechanical shock, vibration, and high-temperature operational life testing. Panasonic's automotive film capacitors are designed for DC link, snubber, and filtering applications in EV/HEV powertrains and charging systems. All automotive-qualified capacitors are manufactured under IATF 16949 quality management systems. Panasonic provides comprehensive PPAP documentation support for automotive customers, including material composition declarations, process flow diagrams, and control plans.
faqs.decisionGuide: Filter our product listings by automotive qualification, or contact our automotive FAE specialist for component recommendations.

categories.name: Aluminum Electrolytic Capacitors
categories.description: High-reliability aluminum electrolytic capacitors for power supplies, inverters, and industrial applications with extended lifetime options.
categories.longDescription: Panasonic's aluminum electrolytic capacitors represent industry-leading technology in energy storage and power filtering applications, available through authorized distributor BeiLuo. The FR series provides low impedance and high ripple current capability for switching power supplies. The FS series offers extended lifetime up to 10000 hours at 105C for industrial applications. The FM series features compact size for space-constrained designs. The WA series is specifically designed for automotive applications with AEC-Q200 qualification. Our comprehensive selection guide helps you choose between different series based on your lifetime, ripple current, and size requirements. All Panasonic electrolytic capacitors feature advanced sealing technology to minimize electrolyte evaporation and ensure long-term reliability in demanding applications.
series: FR Series - Low Impedance, High Ripple
categories.selectionGuide: Select FR series for high ripple current, FS for extended life, FM for compact size, WA for automotive applications.

faqs.question: What is the difference between FR and FS series capacitors?
faqs.answer: FR and FS series are both high-performance aluminum electrolytic capacitors from Panasonic, but they are optimized for different application requirements. The FR series prioritizes low impedance and high ripple current capability, making it ideal for switching power supply output filtering where ripple current handling is critical. FR series typically offers 20-30% higher ripple current capability than standard series at the same size. The FS series prioritizes extended operational lifetime, rated for 10000 hours at 105C compared to 5000-7000 hours for standard series. FS series uses enhanced electrolyte formulation and improved sealing technology. For high-ripple applications like power supplies, FR is the better choice. For applications requiring maximum reliability and long service life, FS is recommended.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose FR for high-ripple power supply applications; select FS for extended lifetime requirements.

faqs.question: What is the maximum ripple current for Panasonic electrolytic capacitors?
faqs.answer: Ripple current ratings for Panasonic aluminum electrolytic capacitors vary significantly by series and size. The FR low-impedance series offers the highest ripple current capability, with ratings from 0.5A to over 10A RMS depending on capacitance and case size. For example, a 1000uF 25V capacitor in the FR series might have 1.5A ripple current rating at 105C, while the same value in a standard series might be 0.6A. Ripple current capability decreases at higher ambient temperatures - at 85C, the rating is typically 70-80% of the 105C rating. For high-ripple applications, consider using multiple capacitors in parallel or selecting the FR series specifically designed for high ripple current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select FR series for high-ripple applications; parallel capacitors for very high ripple currents.

faqs.question: Can Panasonic electrolytic capacitors be used for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, Panasonic offers automotive-qualified aluminum electrolytic capacitors specifically designed for vehicle applications. The WA series is AEC-Q200 qualified with temperature ratings up to 150C for under-hood applications. These capacitors undergo additional testing including temperature cycling from -40C to +150C, mechanical shock, vibration testing, and high-temperature operational life testing. Standard FR and FS series are not qualified for automotive use and should not be used in safety-critical vehicle applications. For automotive applications, always select AEC-Q200 qualified capacitors and ensure proper derating for reliability. Panasonic provides comprehensive PPAP documentation for automotive customers.
faqs.decisionGuide: For automotive applications, select WA series AEC-Q200 qualified capacitors only.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime of Panasonic electrolytic capacitors?
faqs.answer: Panasonic aluminum electrolytic capacitor lifetime varies by series and operating conditions. Standard series are typically rated for 2000-5000 hours at 105C. The FS long-life series extends this to 10000 hours at 105C. Following the Arrhenius equation, lifetime approximately doubles for every 10C decrease in temperature. For example, an FS series capacitor rated for 10000 hours at 105C can achieve 20000 hours at 95C, 40000 hours at 85C, and 80000 hours at 75C. Voltage derating further extends lifetime - operating at 80% of rated voltage typically doubles the expected life. For critical applications, consider the FS series or implement voltage and temperature derating to maximize capacitor lifetime.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use FS series for extended lifetime; apply voltage and temperature derating for maximum reliability.

faqs.question: How do I read Panasonic capacitor date codes?
faqs.answer: Panasonic aluminum electrolytic capacitors use a standardized date code system for traceability. The date code typically consists of a letter representing the year and a number representing the week of manufacture. For example, date code 'K25' indicates the capacitor was manufactured in week 25 of year K (2024). The letter-to-year mapping follows a 20-year cycle: A=2010, B=2011, ..., K=2024, L=2025, etc. Additional markings on the capacitor include the series designation (FR, FS, FM, WA), capacitance value, voltage rating, and tolerance. Understanding these markings helps verify correct component selection, trace manufacturing batch information, and ensure components are used within their recommended shelf life.
faqs.decisionGuide: Refer to Panasonic's marking guide or contact our FAE team for assistance with date code interpretation.

products.name: 100uF 25V FR Series Capacitor
products.shortDescription: 100uF 25V aluminum electrolytic capacitor, FR series, low impedance, 105C rated, 6.3x11mm radial lead.
descriptionParagraphs: The EEUFR1E101 is a high-performance aluminum electrolytic capacitor from Panasonic's FR series, designed for applications requiring low impedance and high ripple current capability. This 100uF capacitor with 25V rating is ideal for switching power supply output filtering and DC bus applications.

Featuring Panasonic's advanced low-impedance electrolyte formulation, this capacitor delivers excellent ripple current handling with reduced self-heating. The robust construction includes a pressure relief vent and high-quality rubber seal for long-term reliability.

The compact 6.3mm diameter x 11mm length case size enables high-density PCB layouts while providing adequate thermal performance. This capacitor is RoHS compliant and suitable for consumer electronics, industrial controls, and power supply applications.
features: Low impedance for high ripple current

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: In my experience supporting power supply designs, the EEUFR1E101 is an excellent choice for low-voltage DC-DC converters and small switching power supplies. The FR series' low impedance characteristic significantly reduces output voltage ripple compared to standard series capacitors. I frequently recommend this part for 12V to 24V rail filtering where space is limited but performance cannot be compromised. The 6.3x11mm size offers good manufacturability while the 0.24A ripple current rating handles moderate load currents effectively. I typically recommend operating at 70-80% of rated voltage to maximize lifetime. For high-frequency switching applications above 100kHz, the low ESR of the FR series provides superior filtering performance.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent low-impedance performance for compact power supply designs

alternativeParts.reason: Extended lifetime version of same series for higher reliability requirements
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications requiring longer operational life with same electrical characteristics

alternativeParts.reason: FS series offers double the lifetime for maximum reliability applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Long-life applications where extended service life is more critical than lowest ESR

companionParts.description: Power relay for power supply control circuits

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime at different operating temperatures?
faqs.answer: The EEUFR1E101 is rated for 5000 hours at 105C. Following the Arrhenius equation, lifetime approximately doubles for every 10C decrease in temperature. Expected lifetimes: 10000 hours at 95C, 20000 hours at 85C, 40000 hours at 75C, and 80000 hours at 65C. These calculations assume rated voltage and ripple current. Voltage derating further extends lifetime - operating at 80% of rated voltage (20V) typically doubles the expected life. For example, at 85C and 20V, expected lifetime could exceed 40000 hours. For maximum reliability, design for at least 2x the required service life.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use temperature and voltage derating to maximize capacitor lifetime in your application.

faqs.question: How much ripple current can this capacitor handle?
faqs.answer: The EEUFR1E101 has a rated ripple current of 0.24A rms at 100kHz and 105C ambient temperature. At lower temperatures, higher ripple currents are permissible: approximately 0.30A at 85C, 0.38A at 65C, and 0.48A at 45C. The ripple current rating is frequency-dependent - higher frequencies allow increased ripple current due to reduced effective impedance. At 10kHz, the rating is approximately 0.18A. Exceeding the ripple current rating causes internal heating, accelerating electrolyte evaporation and reducing lifetime. Always calculate expected ripple current and ensure adequate thermal management with proper airflow.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate expected ripple current and verify thermal design for reliable long-term operation.

faqs.question: What is the ESR of this capacitor?
faqs.answer: The EEUFR1E101 has a maximum ESR of 0.60 ohm at 20C and 100kHz. ESR varies with temperature and frequency. At -40C, ESR can be 3-5 times higher than at room temperature. At 105C, ESR is typically 20-30% lower than at 20C. ESR also decreases with increasing frequency - at 10kHz, ESR is approximately 2-3 times higher than at 100kHz. For switching power supply applications, the ESR at the switching frequency is critical for calculating output voltage ripple and capacitor heating. Lower ESR results in less heating and better filtering performance. The FR series is specifically designed for low ESR.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider ESR at your operating frequency and temperature when calculating ripple voltage and thermal performance.

faqs.question: Can this capacitor be used in parallel with others?
faqs.answer: Yes, connecting multiple capacitors in parallel is a common practice to increase total capacitance and reduce equivalent ESR. When paralleling capacitors of the same value, the total capacitance is the sum of individual capacitances, and ESR is reduced proportionally. For best results, use identical part numbers and place capacitors close together with symmetrical PCB layout. Two EEUFR1E101 in parallel provide 200uF total capacitance with ESR reduced to approximately 0.30 ohm. Current sharing is naturally balanced when using identical capacitors. Parallel configuration is often more cost-effective than selecting a single larger capacitor.
faqs.decisionGuide: Parallel multiple capacitors for increased capacitance and reduced ESR; use symmetrical layout for current sharing.

faqs.question: What is the leakage current specification?
faqs.answer: The maximum leakage current for the EEUFR1E101 is specified as 0.01CV or 3uA, whichever is greater, measured after 2 minutes at rated voltage. For this 100uF 25V capacitor: 0.01 x 100uF x 25V = 25uA maximum leakage current. Typical leakage current is much lower, often below 10uA at 25C. Leakage current increases with temperature - approximately doubling for every 10C rise. At maximum rated temperature (105C), leakage current may approach the maximum specification. High leakage current indicates capacitor aging or damage. For most applications, leakage current is negligible and does not affect circuit performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Measure leakage current during system testing to monitor capacitor health over time.

faqs.question: What is the recommended soldering profile?
faqs.answer: Panasonic recommends standard lead-free wave soldering or hand soldering for radial lead capacitors. For hand soldering, use a temperature-controlled iron set to 350C maximum with contact time limited to 3-5 seconds per lead. Excessive heat or prolonged soldering can cause thermal shock and damage the capacitor. Always ensure proper heat sinking of leads during soldering by using a heatsink clip or tweezers. After soldering, allow the capacitor to cool naturally without forced cooling to prevent thermal stress. For wave soldering, follow standard profiles with preheating to avoid thermal shock. Do not wash capacitors with halogenated solvents.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow recommended soldering profiles and avoid exceeding temperature limits to prevent thermal damage.

products.name: 470uF 35V FR Series Capacitor
products.shortDescription: 470uF 35V aluminum electrolytic capacitor, FR series, low impedance, 105C rated, 10x12.5mm radial lead.
descriptionParagraphs: The EEUFR1V471 is a high-capacitance aluminum electrolytic capacitor from Panasonic's FR series, designed for applications requiring high energy storage and low impedance. This 470uF capacitor with 35V rating is ideal for 24V power supply filtering and DC bus applications.

Featuring Panasonic's advanced low-impedance electrolyte formulation, this capacitor delivers excellent ripple current handling with minimal self-heating. The robust construction includes a pressure relief vent and high-quality sealing system for extended operational life.

The 10mm diameter x 12.5mm length case size provides excellent volumetric efficiency for medium-power applications. This capacitor is RoHS compliant and suitable for industrial power supplies, motor drives, and LED driver applications.
features: Low impedance for high ripple current

faeReview.author: Sarah Johnson
faeReview.title: FAE Manager - Industrial Applications
faeReview.content: The EEUFR1V471 is one of my most frequently recommended capacitors for 24V industrial power supplies. The 35V rating provides excellent voltage margin for 24V systems, and the 470uF capacitance offers good energy storage for moderate power applications. In my experience with industrial designs, this capacitor handles ripple currents up to 0.79A effectively while maintaining low ESR. The 10x12.5mm size provides good thermal performance with adequate surface area for heat dissipation. I typically see this part used in motor drive power supplies, PLC power modules, and industrial control systems. The FR series' reliability track record in industrial environments is excellent.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable choice for 24V industrial power supplies with good voltage margin

alternativeParts.reason: FS series offers double the lifetime for critical industrial applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Industrial equipment requiring maximum reliability and extended service life

alternativeParts.reason: Higher voltage rating for applications with voltage transients or requiring additional margin
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications with voltage spikes or requiring higher voltage derating margin

companionParts.description: Power relay for power supply control

faqs.question: What applications is this capacitor best suited for?
faqs.answer: The EEUFR1V471 is best suited for 24V DC power supply applications where higher voltage margin is desired. The 35V rating provides excellent voltage derating for 24V systems, significantly extending capacitor lifetime. For 24V systems, operating at 24V provides 31% voltage derating. Common applications include industrial control power supplies, motor drive DC bus filtering, LED lighting drivers, and audio amplifier power supplies. The 470uF capacitance is well-suited for medium-power applications up to approximately 150W output power in switching supplies. The low ESR makes it particularly suitable for applications where output voltage ripple must be minimized.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for 24V systems; consider higher capacitance for higher power applications.

faqs.question: How does the higher voltage rating affect lifetime?
faqs.answer: The 35V rating provides significant lifetime advantages when operated at lower voltages. Operating at 24V (69% of rated voltage) can extend lifetime by 3-5x compared to operating at full rated voltage. This follows the inverse power law relationship where lifetime is approximately proportional to (Vrated/Voperating)^7. For example, a capacitor rated for 5000 hours at 35V can achieve 15000-25000 hours when operated at 24V, depending on temperature. This makes the EEUFR1V471 an excellent choice for 24V applications where maximum reliability and lifetime are required. The additional voltage margin also provides protection against voltage transients.
faqs.decisionGuide: Take advantage of voltage derating for 24V applications to maximize lifetime.

faqs.question: What is the surge voltage rating?
faqs.answer: The EEUFR1V471 has a surge voltage rating of 40V (1.15 x rated voltage) for short-duration transients. This allows the capacitor to handle occasional voltage spikes above the normal operating voltage without damage. However, sustained operation above the rated voltage will accelerate aging and reduce lifetime. For applications with frequent voltage transients, consider using a higher voltage rated capacitor or adding external protection such as TVS diodes. The capacitor also features a pressure relief vent that opens if internal pressure becomes excessive, preventing catastrophic failure. Always design for normal operation within the rated voltage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use surge rating for occasional transients only; consider higher voltage rating for sustained overvoltage conditions.

faqs.question: Can this capacitor be used for outdoor applications?
faqs.answer: The EEUFR1V471 can be used in outdoor applications with proper enclosure and protection. The -55C to +105C temperature range covers most outdoor environments. However, the capacitor requires protection from direct exposure to rain, humidity, and corrosive atmospheres. For outdoor installations, use an appropriate enclosure with adequate ventilation for heat dissipation. Consider temperature cycling effects - daily temperature variations can cause pressure changes inside the capacitor. In high-humidity environments, conformal coating of the PCB assembly is recommended to prevent corrosion of the leads and surrounding circuitry. The extended low temperature rating of -55C makes it suitable for cold climate applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use appropriate enclosure and conformal coating for outdoor applications; ensure adequate thermal management.

faqs.question: What is the self-heating at rated ripple current?
faqs.answer: At the rated ripple current of 0.79A rms, the EEUFR1V471 experiences self-heating due to power dissipation in the ESR. The temperature rise can be calculated as dT = I^2 x ESR x Rth, where I is ripple current, ESR is equivalent series resistance, and Rth is thermal resistance. For this capacitor, at rated ripple current and 105C ambient, the core temperature will be approximately 105-110C. At lower ambient temperatures, the self-heating remains similar but provides more margin to the maximum rating. For reliable long-term operation, keep the core temperature below 95C when possible. Use multiple capacitors in parallel to distribute ripple current and reduce individual capacitor heating.
faqs.decisionGuide: Monitor capacitor temperature during testing; parallel capacitors for high-ripple applications to reduce heating.

faqs.question: How does this compare to standard electrolytic capacitors?
faqs.answer: Compared to standard aluminum electrolytic capacitors, the FR series offers significantly better performance in key areas. The ESR is typically 30-50% lower than standard series, resulting in less heating and better filtering. The impedance at high frequency (100kHz) is much lower, making FR series ideal for switching power supplies. The ripple current capability is 2-3x higher than standard series of the same size. While the FR series may have a slightly higher cost, the improved performance often allows using smaller capacitors or fewer parallel units, offsetting the cost difference. The lifetime is comparable to standard series, with FS series available when extended life is required.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider FR series for high-performance applications; the improved specifications often justify the modest cost premium.

products.name: 1000uF 25V FS Series Long Life Capacitor
products.shortDescription: 1000uF 25V aluminum electrolytic capacitor, FS series, long life 10000h at 105C, 12.5x20mm radial lead.
descriptionParagraphs: The EEUFS1E102 is a high-capacitance long-life aluminum electrolytic capacitor from Panasonic's FS series, designed for applications requiring maximum reliability and extended service life. This 1000uF capacitor with 25V rating provides 10000 hours lifetime at 105C, double the standard series rating.

Featuring Panasonic's advanced long-life electrolyte formulation and improved sealing technology, this capacitor delivers exceptional operational life with reliable performance. The robust construction ensures minimal electrolyte evaporation over the extended lifetime.

The 12.5mm diameter x 20mm length case size provides excellent volumetric efficiency for high-capacitance applications. This capacitor is ideal for industrial power supplies, UPS systems, and applications requiring 15+ year service life.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The EEUFS1E102 is my go-to recommendation for applications requiring maximum reliability and long service life. The 10000 hour rating at 105C translates to 20+ years in typical industrial environments with proper derating. I frequently specify this part for UPS systems, industrial controls, and infrastructure equipment where maintenance access is difficult. The FS series' improved sealing technology significantly reduces electrolyte evaporation compared to standard series. While the cost is higher than standard capacitors, the extended lifetime often eliminates the need for replacement, reducing total cost of ownership. For critical applications, the FS series provides peace of mind.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent choice for long-life industrial and infrastructure applications

products.name: 1000uF 50V FR Series High Voltage Capacitor
products.shortDescription: 1000uF 50V aluminum electrolytic capacitor, FR series, low impedance, 105C rated, 12.5x25mm radial lead.
descriptionParagraphs: The EEUFR1H102 is a high-voltage high-capacitance aluminum electrolytic capacitor from Panasonic's FR series, designed for 48V power supply applications and high-voltage DC bus filtering. This 1000uF capacitor with 50V rating provides excellent energy storage with low impedance characteristics.

Featuring Panasonic's advanced low-impedance electrolyte formulation, this capacitor delivers high ripple current capability with minimal self-heating. The robust construction includes a pressure relief vent and high-quality sealing for reliable operation.

The 12.5mm diameter x 25mm length case size provides excellent volumetric efficiency for high-power applications. This capacitor is ideal for 48V telecom power supplies, industrial motor drives, and high-voltage LED drivers.
features: Low impedance for high ripple current

faeReview.author: David Wang
faeReview.title: FAE - Industrial Power
faeReview.content: The EEUFR1H102 is an excellent solution for 48V power supply applications. The 50V rating provides good voltage margin for 48V systems, and the 1000uF capacitance offers substantial energy storage. I've used this capacitor in telecom rectifiers and industrial 48V bus systems with excellent results. The FR series' low ESR minimizes heating even at high ripple currents. For 48V applications, this capacitor provides the right balance of voltage rating, capacitance, and size. The 12.5x25mm package is manageable for most PCB layouts while providing good thermal performance.
faeReview.highlight: Ideal for 48V power supplies with good voltage margin and high capacitance

categories.name: Film Capacitors
categories.description: High-performance metallized film capacitors for AC filtering, snubber circuits, and power electronics applications.
categories.longDescription: Panasonic's film capacitors provide excellent AC performance and self-healing characteristics for demanding power electronics applications, available through authorized distributor BeiLuo. The ECQ-E series offers metallized polyester film capacitors for general-purpose applications. The ECQ-P series provides metallized polypropylene capacitors for high-frequency and pulse applications. The EZP series features DC link capacitors for inverter and power converter applications. Our selection guide helps you choose between polyester and polypropylene dielectrics based on your temperature, frequency, and stability requirements. All Panasonic film capacitors feature self-healing properties that enhance reliability and safety in critical applications.
categories.selectionGuide: Select ECQ-E for general purpose, ECQ-P for high frequency, EZP for DC link applications.

faqs.question: What is the difference between polyester and polypropylene film capacitors?
faqs.answer: Polyester (PET) and polypropylene (PP) film capacitors have different characteristics suited to different applications. Polyester capacitors (ECQ-E series) offer higher capacitance density and lower cost, making them ideal for general-purpose coupling, decoupling, and timing applications. They have moderate temperature stability and are suitable for temperatures up to 125C. Polypropylene capacitors (ECQ-P series) provide superior electrical characteristics including lower dissipation factor, better capacitance stability over temperature, and higher insulation resistance. They are preferred for high-frequency applications, precision circuits, and pulse applications. Polypropylene is the better choice for snubber circuits, resonant circuits, and high-frequency filtering.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose polyester for cost-sensitive general applications; select polypropylene for high-frequency or precision applications.

faqs.question: What is self-healing in film capacitors?
faqs.answer: Self-healing is a characteristic of metallized film capacitors where minor dielectric defects are automatically repaired during operation. When a localized breakdown occurs, the thin metal electrode layer vaporizes around the defect site, isolating the fault and restoring the capacitor's functionality. This process occurs almost instantaneously and is typically undetectable in normal operation. Self-healing enhances capacitor reliability by preventing catastrophic failures and extending operational life. Panasonic film capacitors are designed with optimized metallization patterns that promote effective self-healing while maintaining capacitance stability. This makes them particularly suitable for high-reliability applications where failure is not acceptable.
faqs.decisionGuide: Self-healing makes metallized film capacitors ideal for high-reliability and safety-critical applications.

faqs.question: Can film capacitors replace electrolytic capacitors?
faqs.answer: Film capacitors can replace electrolytic capacitors in many applications, but there are important considerations. Film capacitors offer advantages: much longer lifetime (100,000+ hours), no polarity constraints, lower ESR at high frequencies, and better stability over temperature. However, film capacitors have lower capacitance density - they are physically larger for the same capacitance value. They are also typically more expensive per microfarad. For DC link applications, snubber circuits, and AC filtering, film capacitors are often preferred. For bulk energy storage and high-capacitance filtering, electrolytic capacitors remain more cost-effective. Many designs use both: electrolytics for bulk capacitance and film capacitors for high-frequency filtering.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider film capacitors for high-frequency, long-life, or AC applications; electrolytics for bulk capacitance.

faqs.question: What are typical applications for film capacitors?
faqs.answer: Film capacitors excel in applications requiring stable capacitance, low losses, and high reliability. Common applications include: AC filtering and power factor correction in industrial equipment; snubber circuits for IGBT and MOSFET protection in motor drives and inverters; DC link capacitors in power converters and solar inverters; EMI/RFI suppression filters; resonant circuits in induction heating and welding equipment; timing and coupling circuits in audio equipment; and pulse discharge applications. Film capacitors are particularly valued in applications where long service life is required, such as renewable energy installations and industrial equipment with 20+ year expected life.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use film capacitors for AC applications, snubber circuits, and where long lifetime is critical.

faqs.question: What voltage ratings are available for Panasonic film capacitors?
faqs.answer: Panasonic film capacitors are available in a wide range of voltage ratings to suit different applications. The ECQ-E polyester series offers ratings from 50V to 630V DC, covering most general-purpose applications. The ECQ-P polypropylene series provides ratings from 100V to 2000V DC, suitable for high-voltage applications. The EZP DC link series offers high-capacitance options with ratings from 450V to 1100V DC for inverter and power converter applications. AC voltage ratings are typically 50-70% of DC ratings. When selecting voltage rating, apply appropriate derating (typically 20-30%) for reliability. For AC applications, ensure the peak AC voltage does not exceed the DC rating.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select voltage rating with 20-30% derating; ensure AC peak voltage is within DC rating.

products.name: 0.01uF 400V Film Capacitor
products.shortDescription: 0.01uF 400V metallized polyester film capacitor, ECQ-E series, 10% tolerance, 105C rated, radial lead.
descriptionParagraphs: The ECQE4103KF is a high-quality metallized polyester film capacitor from Panasonic's ECQ-E series, designed for general-purpose applications requiring stable capacitance and reliable performance. This 0.01uF capacitor with 400V rating is ideal for coupling, decoupling, and timing circuits.

Featuring Panasonic's advanced metallized film technology, this capacitor provides self-healing characteristics that enhance long-term reliability. The polyester dielectric offers good capacitance stability and moderate temperature characteristics suitable for most electronic applications.

The compact radial lead package enables easy PCB mounting and is compatible with automatic insertion equipment. This capacitor is RoHS compliant and suitable for consumer electronics, industrial controls, and power supply applications.

faeReview.author: James Wilson
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Passive Components
faeReview.content: The ECQE4103KF is a versatile film capacitor that I frequently recommend for general-purpose applications. The 400V rating provides excellent voltage margin for 120V/240V AC line applications when used in EMI filters or snubber circuits. The self-healing characteristic of metallized film construction provides enhanced reliability compared to foil types. In my experience, this capacitor performs well in power supply EMI filters, motor drive snubbers, and general coupling applications. The 10% tolerance is suitable for most applications, though tighter tolerances are available if needed. The compact 5mm lead spacing fits well in dense PCB layouts.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable general-purpose film capacitor with self-healing properties

alternativeParts.reason: Polypropylene dielectric provides better high-frequency performance and higher voltage rating
alternativeParts.useCase: High-frequency applications or where higher voltage margin is required

faqs.question: What is the temperature coefficient of this capacitor?
faqs.answer: The ECQE4103KF uses polyester (PET) dielectric which has a temperature coefficient of approximately +200 to +500 ppm/C. This means the capacitance increases slightly with temperature. Over the operating range of -40C to +105C, the capacitance can vary by approximately +/- 5% from the nominal value. This moderate temperature stability is suitable for most general-purpose applications. For applications requiring better temperature stability, consider the ECQ-P polypropylene series which has a near-zero temperature coefficient and typically varies less than +/- 2% over temperature. The temperature coefficient should be considered in precision timing circuits or applications where capacitance stability is critical.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider polypropylene series for applications requiring better temperature stability.

faqs.question: Can this capacitor be used for AC applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, the ECQE4103KF can be used for AC applications with appropriate voltage derating. The 400V DC rating typically corresponds to approximately 200-250V AC RMS maximum, depending on the application and safety standards. For AC applications, ensure the peak AC voltage (1.414 x Vrms) does not exceed the DC rating. For 120V AC line applications, this capacitor provides adequate margin. For 240V AC applications, consider using a higher voltage rated part or two capacitors in series with balancing resistors. Film capacitors are non-polarized and can handle AC without the polarity constraints of electrolytic capacitors. Always verify compliance with applicable safety standards for AC line-connected applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure peak AC voltage is within DC rating; use higher voltage parts for 240V AC applications.

categories.name: Electromechanical Relays
categories.description: High-reliability electromechanical relays for power switching, control systems, and automotive applications with various contact configurations.
categories.longDescription: Panasonic's electromechanical relays provide reliable switching solutions for diverse applications from signal level to high-power loads, available through authorized distributor BeiLuo. The ALDP series offers compact PCB power relays for industrial and consumer applications. The DSP series provides high-capacity power relays for motor control and HVAC systems. The TQ series features automotive relays with enhanced vibration resistance. Our comprehensive selection guide helps you choose between different contact materials, coil voltages, and switching capacities based on your load characteristics and environmental requirements. All Panasonic relays feature high-quality contact materials and robust construction for long electrical life.
series: ALDP Series - Compact PCB Power Relays
DSP Series - High Capacity Power Relays
categories.selectionGuide: Select ALDP for compact PCB mounting, DSP for high current, TQ for automotive applications.

faqs.question: What is the difference between SPST, SPDT, and DPDT relay configurations?
faqs.answer: Relay contact configurations determine how the relay switches circuits. SPST (Single Pole Single Throw) is the simplest configuration with one set of contacts that either open or close a single circuit - like a simple on/off switch. SPDT (Single Pole Double Throw) has one set of contacts that can switch between two circuits - like a toggle switch that selects between two outputs. DPDT (Double Pole Double Throw) has two sets of SPDT contacts that switch simultaneously - useful for reversing motor direction or switching two circuits at once. The choice depends on your application: SPST for simple on/off control, SPDT for selecting between two circuits, and DPDT for more complex switching requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose SPST for simple switching, SPDT for selecting between circuits, DPDT for simultaneous dual switching.

faqs.question: What contact material should I choose for my application?
faqs.answer: Panasonic relays offer different contact materials optimized for various load types. AgSnO2 (Silver Tin Oxide) is the most common material, offering excellent resistance to welding and material transfer, making it ideal for inductive loads like motors and solenoids. AgCdO (Silver Cadmium Oxide) provides good arc quenching and is suitable for moderate inductive loads, though it contains cadmium which may have environmental restrictions. Au-clad (Gold-clad) contacts provide excellent low-level switching performance and are ideal for dry circuits, signal switching, and precious metal compatibility. For most power applications, AgSnO2 is recommended. For signal or low-level switching, Au-clad is preferred.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use AgSnO2 for inductive power loads; Au-clad for signal and low-level switching.

faqs.question: How do I calculate the required coil voltage for a relay?
faqs.answer: The coil voltage of a relay must match your control circuit voltage for proper operation. Panasonic relays are available in standard coil voltages: 5V, 12V, 24V, and 48V DC are most common, with AC coil options also available. Select a coil voltage that matches your control system voltage. Consider the coil power consumption - higher voltage coils typically draw less current for the same power. Ensure your control circuit can provide the required coil current (typically 20-100mA for PCB relays, higher for power relays). Include a flyback diode across the coil for DC relays to protect your switching transistor from inductive kickback. For sensitive applications, consider relays with low coil power consumption.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match relay coil voltage to your control circuit; include flyback protection for DC coils.

faqs.question: What is electrical life vs mechanical life in relays?
faqs.answer: Relay life is specified in two categories: mechanical life and electrical life. Mechanical life refers to the number of operations the relay can perform with no load (or minimal load) applied - this is typically much higher, often 10-50 million operations. Electrical life refers to the number of operations under rated load conditions - this is typically lower, ranging from 100,000 to 1,000,000 operations depending on the load type and current. The electrical life is shorter because switching actual loads causes contact arcing, erosion, and material transfer. Resistive loads provide the longest electrical life, while inductive loads reduce life due to arcing. Capacitive loads can cause high inrush currents that severely reduce contact life. Always consider the load type when evaluating relay life specifications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use electrical life rating for loaded applications; mechanical life is for dry switching only.

faqs.question: Can Panasonic relays be used for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, Panasonic offers automotive-qualified relays specifically designed for vehicle applications. The TQ series automotive relays are designed to meet automotive standards including vibration resistance, temperature cycling, and electrical load requirements. These relays feature enhanced contact materials and construction to withstand the harsh automotive environment. Standard industrial relays like the ALDP and DSP series are not qualified for automotive use and should not be used in safety-critical vehicle applications. For automotive applications, always select relays specifically designed and qualified for automotive use. Panasonic provides comprehensive documentation and testing data for automotive customers.
faqs.decisionGuide: For automotive applications, select TQ series automotive-qualified relays only.

products.name: SPST Power Relay 12V DC
products.shortDescription: SPST-NO power relay, 12V DC coil, 10A switching, ALDP series, PCB mounting, AgSnO2 contacts.
descriptionParagraphs: The ALDP112 is a compact PCB power relay from Panasonic's ALDP series, designed for switching loads up to 10A in industrial and consumer applications. This SPST-NO (Single Pole Single Throw, Normally Open) relay features a 12V DC coil and AgSnO2 contacts for reliable switching of resistive and inductive loads.

Featuring Panasonic's high-quality contact materials and robust construction, this relay provides excellent electrical life and reliable operation. The compact PCB mounting package saves space while maintaining high switching capacity.
The 12V DC coil is compatible with standard control systems and industrial PLCs. This relay is suitable for motor control, HVAC systems, lighting control, and industrial automation applications.

faeReview.author: Robert Taylor
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Controls
faeReview.content: The ALDP112 is one of my go-to recommendations for general-purpose power switching in industrial controls. The compact 15.5x10.5mm footprint fits well in dense PCB layouts while providing 10A switching capability. The AgSnO2 contact material handles inductive loads much better than standard silver contacts - I've seen significantly fewer welding issues with this material. The 400mW coil power is efficient and easy to drive from standard logic or PLC outputs. I typically recommend adding a flyback diode across the coil to protect the driving circuit. For motor loads, I suggest derating to 5-7A to extend contact life. Overall, this is a reliable, cost-effective relay for industrial applications.
faeReview.highlight: Compact and reliable power relay with excellent inductive load handling

alternativeParts.reason: Higher current and SPDT configuration for more demanding applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Higher current loads or applications requiring SPDT switching

companionParts.description: High capacity relay for larger loads

faqs.question: What is the inrush current capability of this relay?
faqs.answer: The ALDP112 can handle inrush currents significantly higher than its continuous rating. For resistive loads, it can switch up to 10A continuously. For inductive loads like motors and solenoids, the inrush current can be 5-10x the running current. The AgSnO2 contact material is specifically chosen for its ability to handle these inrush currents without welding. However, for high inrush applications (like tungsten lamp loads or large capacitor banks), consider using a relay with higher contact rating or implementing soft-start circuits. The relay can typically handle 30A inrush for a few milliseconds. Always verify actual inrush currents in your application and select appropriate protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high inrush loads, consider higher rated relays or soft-start circuits.

faqs.question: How do I protect the relay coil in my circuit?
faqs.answer: Relay coils are inductive loads that generate a high voltage spike (back EMF) when de-energized. This spike can damage driving transistors or microcontrollers. Always include a flyback protection diode across the coil for DC relays. Connect the diode cathode to the positive coil terminal and anode to the negative terminal. A standard 1N4007 or similar rectifier diode is suitable for most applications. For faster relay release times, consider using a zener diode in series with the flyback diode, or a TVS diode. The ALDP112's 360 ohm coil draws approximately 33mA at 12V, so ensure your driving circuit can supply this current continuously.
faqs.decisionGuide: Always use flyback diode protection for DC relay coils to prevent voltage spikes.

products.name: SPST Power Relay 24V DC
products.shortDescription: SPST-NO power relay, 24V DC coil, 10A switching, ALDP series, PCB mounting, AgSnO2 contacts.
descriptionParagraphs: The ALDP124 is a compact PCB power relay from Panasonic's ALDP series, designed for switching loads up to 10A in industrial and consumer applications. This SPST-NO relay features a 24V DC coil and AgSnO2 contacts for reliable switching of resistive and inductive loads.

The 24V DC coil is compatible with standard industrial control systems and PLCs. This relay is suitable for motor control, HVAC systems, lighting control, and industrial automation applications where 24V control voltage is standard.
Featuring Panasonic's high-quality contact materials and robust construction, this relay provides excellent electrical life and reliable operation in demanding industrial environments.
features: 24V DC coil for industrial control systems

faeReview.author: Robert Taylor
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Controls
faeReview.content: The ALDP124 is the 24V version of the popular ALDP112 relay. In industrial environments, 24V control voltage is the standard, making this relay ideal for PLC-based control systems. The electrical characteristics are identical to the 12V version, providing the same reliable 10A switching capability. I frequently recommend this relay for factory automation projects where 24V is the control voltage standard. The AgSnO2 contacts handle motor loads and inductive switching much better than standard relays. The compact size allows for high-density relay boards in control panels. Always include a flyback diode when driving from PLC outputs.
faeReview.highlight: Standard 24V relay for industrial PLC control systems

products.name: SPDT High Capacity Relay 24V DC
products.shortDescription: SPDT power relay, 24V DC coil, 16A switching, DSP series, PCB mounting, AgSnO2 contacts for high-current applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The DSP1A-DC24V is a high-capacity PCB power relay from Panasonic's DSP series, designed for switching loads up to 16A in demanding industrial applications. This SPDT relay features a 24V DC coil and AgSnO2 contacts for reliable switching of high-current resistive and inductive loads.

The SPDT configuration provides versatility for applications requiring switching between two circuits or normally-closed operation. The 16A rating makes this relay suitable for larger motors, heaters, and industrial equipment.

Featuring robust construction and high-quality AgSnO2 contacts, this relay provides long electrical life even under demanding switching conditions. The DSP series is designed for industrial applications requiring higher current capacity than standard relays.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: FAE - Industrial Power
faeReview.content: The DSP1A-DC24V is my recommendation when higher current capacity is needed. The 16A rating handles larger motors and heaters that exceed the capacity of standard 10A relays. The SPDT configuration is useful for applications requiring both normally-open and normally-closed contacts. I've used this relay in industrial motor control panels and HVAC systems with excellent reliability. The AgSnO2 contacts are essential for motor loads - they resist welding much better than standard contacts. The coil power is higher at 900mW, so ensure your driving circuit can supply sufficient current. For very large motors, consider using this relay to control a contactor rather than switching the motor directly.
faeReview.highlight: High-capacity relay for demanding industrial applications

products.name: Signal Relay 5V DC
products.shortDescription: DPDT signal relay, 5V DC coil, 2A switching, TQ series, compact SMD package for signal switching applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The TQ2-L2-5V is a compact DPDT signal relay from Panasonic's TQ series, designed for low-level signal switching and telecommunications applications. This relay features a 5V DC coil and DPDT contacts capable of switching up to 2A.

The compact SMD package enables high-density PCB layouts while providing reliable signal switching. This relay is ideal for audio switching, test equipment, telecommunications, and other applications requiring clean signal switching.

Featuring low contact resistance and excellent high-frequency characteristics, this relay provides reliable performance in sensitive electronic circuits. The TQ series is known for its compact size and reliable operation.
features: Compact SMD package for high-density layouts

faeReview.author: Sarah Johnson
faeReview.title: FAE - Signal Processing
faeReview.content: The TQ2-L2-5V is an excellent choice for signal switching applications where compact size is important. The DPDT configuration allows switching two signals simultaneously, which is useful for stereo audio or differential signals. The Au-clad contacts provide reliable low-level switching without contact oxidation issues. I've used this relay in audio equipment, test instruments, and telecommunications equipment with excellent results. The 5V coil is compatible with modern digital circuits and microcontrollers. The SMD package enables compact designs, though hand soldering requires care due to the small size. For signal applications, this relay provides clean switching with minimal contact bounce.
faeReview.highlight: Compact signal relay for audio and telecommunications
